behavior_summary: This script implements the in-game behavior for a wall lamp object. When the object is examined (U8P_LAMP2::look), it prepares the item and emits a short descriptive text 'wall lamp '.

When the object is used/activated, it reads the lamp's world coordinates (X, Y, Z), adjusts the Y coordinate downward by 0x20 (32 units), and calls an engine/free routine (FREE::2411) with the adjusted coordinates, a mode/flag of 1, a code 0x25E, and the lamp object data — inferred to spawn or trigger a lamp-related spatial effect (light, flame, or visual animation) at that location.
notable_control_flow: Event-driven: handlers run as short processes in response to actor interactions (Event 0 = U8P_LAMP2::look/examine, Event 1 = use/activate).
U8P_LAMP2::look sets local item info/exclusion flags and calls Item::bark(item_ptr, "wall lamp ") to present the description, then suspends/returns.
U8P_LAMP2::use queries Item::getZ/getY/getX in that order, subtracts 0x20 from the Y return value, and then calls FREE::2411(x, y-0x20, z, 1, 0x25E, item_ptr).
open_questions: What exact effect FREE::2411 implements for code 0x25E (visual light, flame, persistent object, or transient effect)? This is inferred but not explicit.
Why is the Y coordinate adjusted by exactly 0x20 (32) units before spawning the effect — to align with sprite origin, place the effect lower on the wall, or for engine coordinate conventions?
What is the meaning of the small temporary/local 'temp' used after the implies/pop in the U8P_LAMP2::look handler (control/flow-only artifact vs. signaling return)?
Are there any global flags or broader state changes associated with toggling the lamp (e.g., toggling light on/off) elsewhere in the system that interact with this script?

main_function.name: U8P_LAMP2::look
main_function.purpose: Handle 'U8P_LAMP2::look' (examine) interactions for the wall lamp: set up item info/exclusion, and present a short descriptive string 'wall lamp ' via Item::bark to the inspecting actor/process.
entry_conditions: Event 0: triggered when a player or actor examines/looks at the lamp (examine/observe interaction).

function_map.name: U8P_LAMP2::look
function_map.role: Examine/U8P_LAMP2::look event handler for the wall lamp; produces the descriptive text and completes the U8P_LAMP2::look interaction.

function_map.name: U8P_LAMP2::use
function_map.role: Use/activate event handler for the wall lamp; collects coordinates, adjusts Y, and invokes an engine routine to spawn or trigger a spatial effect tied to the lamp.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item API used to emit or present a short text message associated with an item/process (used for the U8P_LAMP2::look description).

function_map.name: Item::getX
function_map.role: Return the lamp's X coordinate for spatial operations.

function_map.name: Item::getY
function_map.role: Return the lamp's Y coordinate for spatial operations (subsequently adjusted by -0x20).

function_map.name: Item::getZ
function_map.role: Return the lamp's Z coordinate (height/level) for spatial operations.

function_map.name: FREE::2411
function_map.role: Engine/free routine invoked with coordinates, mode/flag, a type/code (0x25E), and item data; likely creates or triggers the lamp's visual/spatial effect.

external_calls.purpose: Present the short descriptive string 'wall lamp ' to the inspector/process. Used to display the examine text.
Obtain the lamp's Z coordinate (height) to pass as the Z argument to the engine free-routine.
Obtain the lamp's Y coordinate; result is then decremented by 0x20 before being passed to FREE::2411 (position adjustment for effect placement).
Obtain the lamp's X coordinate to pass as the X argument to the engine free-routine.
Invoke an engine routine with (x, y-0x20, z, mode=1, code=0x25E, item_ptr) to create or trigger a spatial effect (inferred: lamp-related light/animation) at the adjusted location.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the in-game behavior for a wall lamp object. When the object is examined (U8P_LAMP2::look), it prepares the item and emits a short descriptive text 'wall lamp '.
